summary: Diane Hendricks discusses her journey from growing up on a dairy farm to co-founding ABC Supply, detailing how she and her husband identified a massive gap in roofing distribution. She shares her philosophies on perseverance, the importance of due diligence in acquisitions, community revitalization in Beloit, and the necessity of dreaming to set life goals.

principles.rationale: Once you quit, it is over. Perseverance is non-negotiable.
principles.source_quote: I don't believe in quitting... you quit you're done doesn't matter what it is

principles.statement: Identify and fill the gaps in your industry's supply chain.
principles.rationale: By observing that manufacturers controlled regional distribution and contractors couldn't access diverse products nationally, they realized the need for a nationwide distribution chain.
principles.source_quote: we realized that there needed to be a distribution chain to be able to offer to the customer us the contractor the selection of various manufacturing products Across the Nation

principles.statement: Surround yourself with great people of high integrity.
principles.rationale: The success of a company ultimately comes down to the quality and integrity of the people who make it up.
principles.source_quote: hiring great people surrounding myself with people of good integrity... people always make the difference they make all the difference in the world

principles.statement: You must dream in order to have a goal.
principles.rationale: Without a vision for your future, you won't have a direction or know where you are going in life.
principles.source_quote: you do dream if you don't dream you don't have a goal you don't know where you're going to go

principles.statement: Adding value to a community elevates everyone.
principles.rationale: When you invest in local infrastructure and businesses, it creates a ripple effect that encourages others to improve and thrive.
principles.source_quote: when you start adding value to a community everybody picks up their game

frameworks.name: Acquisition Due Diligence
frameworks.when_to_apply: When evaluating a potential company to purchase or acquire.
steps: Look under the covers more than once to see exactly what you are buying.
Verify that the acquisition makes strict financial sense.
Determine if there is a genuine need for the acquisition, or if you should move elsewhere.
frameworks.source_quote: When you do Acquisitions you learn the word diligence because you've got to look under the covers more than once and see what you're buying to make sure that it makes Financial sense and is there really a need

mental_models.name: The Entrepreneur as a Risk Taker
mental_models.description: Viewing entrepreneurship fundamentally as the willingness to take calculated risks, even when traditional institutions (like banks) shy away from it.
mental_models.example: Her husband Ken called himself an entrepreneur, which initially halted his banking relationships because the bank equated it with being a 'Risk Taker' and refused to do business with them.

mental_models.name: Community as an Ecosystem
mental_models.description: Viewing a city's revitalization as an interconnected system where investing in core infrastructure (like abandoned buildings) breathes life back into the area, attracting secondary businesses like hotels and restaurants.
mental_models.example: Buying the bankrupt Beloit Corporation buildings and turning them into a thriving center with hotels and restaurants, which brought life back to the city.

heuristics: Look under the covers more than once.
Trust your experts within the companies.
If you don't dream, you don't have a goal.

signature_quotes.text: I don't believe in quitting... you quit you're done doesn't matter what it is.
signature_quotes.context: When asked if there was ever a moment she wanted to give up.

signature_quotes.text: When you start adding value to a community everybody picks up their game.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing her investments in revitalizing the city of Beloit.

signature_quotes.text: People always make the difference they make all the difference in the world.
signature_quotes.context: Summarizing the secret to ABC Supply's success.

signature_quotes.text: You do dream if you don't dream you don't have a goal you don't know where you're going to go.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on her childhood dreams of leaving the farm and going into business.

signature_quotes.text: I didn't go to college I had to learn I had to learn what I know you know whether it be hard knocks or by mistake but you're learning you carry that that knowledge with you.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing her lifelong learning process and advice to her children.

anti_patterns.description: Investing in an idea that hasn't been initiated or proven real.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Because 'everybody's got an idea.' An idea is only worth investing in if the entrepreneur has taken it to the next step and actually initiated the product.

anti_patterns.description: Seeking personal recognition by putting your name on buildings.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It is driven by ego rather than purpose. True pleasure comes from seeing the city come back and giving people purpose, not from seeing your own name.

anti_patterns.description: Graduating students without vocational or career knowledge.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It leaves children without the ability to get on with their lives or achieve a quality life, which is why she built a charter school focused on career and tech education.
